sections.paragraph:
ἐξαυτῆς (=ἐξ αὐτῆς τ. ὁδοῦ [DELG s.v. αὐτός; also Frisk s.v. ἐξαυτῆς]; ἐξ αὐτῆς Mk 6:25 v.l.; cp. Philo, Mut. Nom. 142) adv. at once, immediately, soon thereafter (since Theognis 1, 231 Diehl v.l.; Cratinus [V b.c.], Fgm. 34 [I 22 Kock]; Polyb.; Jos., Ant. 7, 122; 15, 186; En 31:2 [corrupt]. Written as one word PLond 893, 6 [40 a.d.]; PRyl 236, 22; PTebt 421, 2; POxy 64, 3 et al.) Mk 6:25; Ac 10:33; 11:11; 21:32; 23:30 (v.l. ἐξ αὐτῶν); Phil 2:23.—M-M.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Theognis Theognis , VI b.c.—List 5

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Kock Kock , s. Com.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 346
